query_normalized: unveiling hidden correlations between processing parameters and mechanical properties in additively manufactured alloys materials science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) by measuring the direct causal link between a specific processing parameter (ultrasound application) and a resulting microstructural property (grain size/density) in an additively manufactured alloy (316L stainless steel), which serves as a foundational mechanism for the mechanical properties the user is investigating.
